RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO SPECIALLY SET A TRIAL DATE PURSUANT TO (A), (D) & (E)

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted as amended: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted per C.C.P. 36(a). Trial is April 2, 2013 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day for hearing summary motions is March 21, 2013. Plaintiffs have stipulated that summary motions may be brought on shortened notice. Summary motions may be brought on 28 days notice, without an additional two days for e-service, with oppositions due 7 court days before the hearing and replies due 3 court days before the hearing. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on all defendants no later than March 8, 2013. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on March 21, 2013. Fact discovery cut-off date is March 15, 2013. Expert discovery cut-off date is March 29, 2013. No later than December 28, 2012, plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than December 28, 2012, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. The parties to meet and confer regarding any other scheduling or other issue which any of them want to have included in the preference order. A further case management conference is set on February 13, 2013 at 1:30 p.m. in Department 503. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 and the date is March 22, 2013 at 10:00 a.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0. The confidential settlement conference statements due by 4p.m. on 3/21. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)
